messages.content: Make a random version scheme and tell me the properties of each one separated with a dot

Version Scheme: X.Y.Z

- X: Represents major changes or updates that may introduce breaking changes or major new features.
- Y: Represents minor changes or updates that include new features or enhancements without breaking existing functionalities.
- Z: Represents patch updates or bug fixes that do not introduce new features, just addressing issues from the previous version.
Add RC, Beta, Alpha, Pre-alpha, Teaser and Demo properties.

Version Scheme: X.Y.Z-RC.Beta-Alpha.Pre-alpha-Teaser-Demo

- X: Represents major changes or updates that may introduce breaking changes or major new features.
- Y: Represents minor changes or updates that include new features or enhancements without breaking existing functionalities.
- Z: Represents patch updates or bug fixes that do not introduce new features, just addressing issues from the previous version.
- RC: Indicates a release candidate, a version that is considered to be stable for release unless significant bugs are found.
- Beta: Indicates a version that is feature complete but may still have bugs to be fixed before the final release.
- Alpha: Indicates a version that is still in development and may have incomplete features or known issues.
- Pre-alpha: Indicates a very early version of the software, likely to have many bugs and missing features.
- Teaser: Indicates a version released for promotional purposes or to showcase a sneak peek of upcoming features.
- Demo: Indicates a version released for demonstration purposes, usually with limited functionality or time-limited features.
Remake the properties' names to shortened down ones

Version Scheme: X.Y.Z-RC-Beta-α-PA-Teaser-Demo

- X: Represents major changes or updates that may introduce breaking changes or major new features.
- Y: Represents minor changes or updates that include new features or enhancements without breaking existing functionalities.
- Z: Represents patch updates or bug fixes that do not introduce new features, just addressing issues from the previous version.
- RC: Release Candidate - a version considered stable for release unless major bugs are found.
- Beta: A feature-complete version with known bugs to be fixed before the final release.
- α: Alpha - a version under development with incomplete features or known issues.
- PA: Pre-Alpha - a very early version likely to have bugs and missing features.
- Teaser: A version released for promotional purposes or to showcase upcoming features.
- Demo: A version released for demonstration purposes with limited functionality or time-limited features.
Remake the Teaser and Demo ones

Version Scheme: X.Y.Z-RC-Beta-α-PA-Ts-Dm

- X: Represents major changes or updates that may introduce breaking changes or major new features.
- Y: Represents minor changes or updates that include new features or enhancements without breaking existing functionalities.
- Z: Represents patch updates or bug fixes that do not introduce new features, just addressing issues from the previous version.
- RC: Release Candidate - a version considered stable for release unless major bugs are found.
- Beta: A feature-complete version with known bugs to be fixed before the final release.
- α: Alpha - a version under development with incomplete features or known issues.
- PA: Pre-Alpha - a very early version likely to have bugs and missing features.
- Ts: Teaser - a version released for promotional purposes or to showcase upcoming features.
- Dm: Demo - a version released for demonstration purposes with limited functionality or time-limited features.
Remake the Beta one.

Version Scheme: X.Y.Z-RC-B-α-PA-Ts-Dm

- X: Represents major changes or updates that may introduce breaking changes or major new features.
- Y: Represents minor changes or updates that include new features or enhancements without breaking existing functionalities.
- Z: Represents patch updates or bug fixes that do not introduce new features, just addressing issues from the previous version.
- RC: Release Candidate - a version considered stable for release unless major bugs are found.
- B: Beta - a version with features complete and known bugs to be fixed before final release.
- α: Alpha - a version under development with incomplete features or known issues.
- PA: Pre-Alpha - a very early version likely to have bugs and missing features.
- Ts: Teaser - a version released for promotional purposes or to showcase upcoming features.
- Dm: Demo - a version released for demonstration purposes with limited functionality or time-limited features.
Make all from RC down into Greek letters

Version Scheme: X.Y.Z-Ψ-Β-α-β-γ-δ

- X: Represents major changes or updates that may introduce breaking changes or major new features.
- Y: Represents minor changes or updates that include new features or enhancements without breaking existing functionalities.
- Z: Represents patch updates or bug fixes that do not introduce new features, just addressing issues from the previous version.
- Ψ: Release Candidate - a version considered stable for release unless major bugs are found.
- Β: Beta - a version with features complete and known bugs to be fixed before final release.
- α: Alpha - a version under development with incomplete features or known issues.
- β: Pre-Alpha - a very early version likely to have bugs and missing features.
- γ: Teaser - a version released for promotional purposes or to showcase upcoming features.
- δ: Demo - a version released for demonstration purposes with limited functionality or time-limited features.
Replace the B with a unique symbol

- Λ: Represents significant changes or updates that may introduce breaking changes or major new features.
- Δ: Represents moderate changes or updates that include new features or enhancements without breaking existing functionalities.
- Γ: Represents minor changes, patch updates, or bug fixes that do not introduce new features, just addressing issues from the previous version.

- ⚙: Beta - a version with features complete and known bugs to be fixed before final release.

- β: Pre-Alpha - a very early version likely to have bugs and missing features.
- γ: Teaser - a version released for promotional purposes or to showcase upcoming features.

Remake the words that represent the letters.

Version Scheme: Lambda.Delta.Gamma-Ψ-⚙-Alpha-Beta-Gamma-Delta

- Lambda: Represents significant changes or updates that may introduce breaking changes or major new features.
- Delta: Represents moderate changes or updates that include new features or enhancements without breaking existing functionalities.
- Gamma: Represents minor changes, patch updates, or bug fixes that do not introduce new features, just addressing issues from the previous version.
- Ψ: Release Candidate - a version considered stable for release unless major bugs are found.
- ⚙: Beta - a version with features complete and known bugs to be fixed before final release.

- Beta: Pre-Alpha - a very early version likely to have bugs and missing features.
- Gamma: Teaser - a version released for promotional purposes or to showcase upcoming features.

Remake the last Gamma into a different letter

Version Scheme: Lambda.Delta.Gamma-Ψ-⚙-Alpha-Beta-Epsilon-Delta

- Lambda: Represents significant changes or updates that may introduce breaking changes or major new features.
- Delta: Represents moderate changes or updates that include new features or enhancements without breaking existing functionalities.
- Gamma: Represents minor changes, patch updates, or bug fixes that do not introduce new features, just addressing issues from the previous version.
- Ψ: Release Candidate - a version considered stable for release unless major bugs are found.
- ⚙: Beta - a version with features complete and known bugs to be fixed before final release.
- Alpha: Alpha - a version under development with incomplete features or known issues.
- Beta: Pre-Alpha - a very early version likely to have bugs and missing features.
- Epsilon: Teaser - a version released for promotional purposes or to showcase upcoming features.
- Delta: Demo - a version released for demonstration purposes with limited functionality or time-limited features.
Add more going down to 0

Version Scheme: Lambda.Delta.Gamma-Epsilon-Dzeta-Eta-Theta-Iota-Ψ-⚙-Alpha-Beta-Epsilon-Delta-0

- Lambda: Represents significant changes or updates that may introduce breaking changes or major new features.
- Delta: Represents moderate changes or updates that include new features or enhancements without breaking existing functionalities.
- Gamma: Represents minor changes, patch updates, or bug fixes that do not introduce new features, just addressing issues from the previous version.
- Epsilon: Represents smaller changes or improvements.
- Dzeta: Represents further minor changes or adjustments.
- Eta: Represents additional minor updates or fixes.
- Theta: Represents very small updates or tweaks.
- Iota: Represents the smallest updates or bug fixes.
- Ψ: Release Candidate - a version considered stable for release unless major bugs are found.
- ⚙: Beta - a version with features complete and known bugs to be fixed before final release.
- Alpha: Alpha - a version under development with incomplete features or known issues.
- Beta: Pre-Alpha - a very early version likely to have bugs and missing features.
- Epsilon: Teaser - a version released for promotional purposes or to showcase upcoming features.
- Delta: Demo - a version released for demonstration purposes with limited functionality or time-limited features.
- 0: Represents the initial version or a significant milestone with no additional changes to the original release.
Make the descriptions Shorter

Version Scheme: L.D.G-Ez-Et-Th-Io-R-B-E-D-0

- L: Significant changes or major updates.
- D: Moderate changes or feature additions.
- G: Minor changes, patch updates, or bug fixes.
- Ez: Smaller changes or improvements.
- Et: Further minor changes or adjustments.
- Th: Additional minor updates or fixes.
- Io: Very small updates or tweaks.
- R: Release Candidate - stable for release.
- B: Beta - features complete with known bugs.
- E: Alpha - under development with incomplete features.
- D: Pre-Alpha - early version with bugs.
- 0: Initial version or milestone.
Replace all letters with greek ones

Version Scheme: Λ.Δ.Γ-Εζ-Ητ-Θ-Ιω-Ρ-Β-Ε-Δ-Σ

- Λ: Significant changes or major updates.
- Δ: Moderate changes or feature additions.
- Γ: Minor changes, patch updates, or bug fixes.
- Εζ: Smaller changes or improvements.
- Ητ: Further minor changes or adjustments.
- Θ: Additional minor updates or fixes.
- Ιω: Very small updates or tweaks.
- Ρ: Release Candidate - stable for release.
- Β: Beta - features complete with known bugs.
- Ε: Alpha - under development with incomplete features.
- Δ: Pre-Alpha - early version with bugs.
- Σ: Initial version or milestone.
